description: Conference email → extract metadata, append to events.md, move to processed/

emails.content:
---
type: email
email-type: conference
date: 2024-03-15
---

IUPAC World Chemistry Congress 2025
Dates: August 10-15, 2025, Ottawa, Canada
Abstract submission deadline: March 31, 2025
Registration deadline: June 30, 2025
Homepage: https://iupac2025.org
Registration: https://iupac2025.org/register
Cost: CAD 1,200 (standard). Student rate: CAD 600.
Topics: computational chemistry, molecular modelling, chemical informatics.

expected_behavior: Extracts: name=IUPAC World Chemistry Congress 2025, dates=2025-08-10–15, location=Ottawa Canada, abstract deadline=2025-03-31, registration deadline=2025-06-30, cost=CAD 1200/600, homepage=https://iupac2025.org. Reads research_interests.md for relevance rubric. Appends table row to events.md. Appends summary block under ## Event Summaries. Re-sorts table by registration deadline ascending. Moves file to 00-Inbox/processed/ via Bash mv. Logs action to 40-Meta/ai-changes.md.

description: Tutor email → summarise, extract decisions, promote to decisions-log.md, flag action items

emails.content:
---
type: email
email-type: tutor
date: 2024-03-16
project: PhD-Ch1-AIM4ML
---

From: Angel Pendes
Subject: QM40 next steps

We agreed to run IQA on the full QM40 dataset before the June meeting.
Decision: use AIMALL for all IQA calculations (consistent with previous chapter).
Action item: send me the benchmark comparison by May 30.
Decision: fixed-fraction 80/20 train/test split for Phase 3.
The DFT level should remain ωB97X-D/6-31G* for now.

expected_behavior: Summarises in 3-5 bullets. Extracts decisions: use AIMALL, 80/20 split, ωB97X-D/6-31G*. Promotes decisions to 10-Projects/PhD-Ch1-AIM4ML/decisions-log.md. Flags action item (benchmark comparison by May 30) explicitly in final report — does not silently drop it. Moves file to 00-Inbox/processed/. Logs to ai-changes.md.

description: Loose notes — Paper type moves to 20-Sources/; Concept type proposes Galaxy skeleton (no write)

loose_notes.first_200_chars: Bader's foundational AIM monograph. Key framework for topological analysis of electron density.
Topological analysis of the electron density field. Critical points: (3,-3) nuclei, (3,-1) BCPs, (3,+1) RCPs. Gradient vector field defines atomic basins.

expected_behavior: bader1990-atoms-in-molecules.md (type=Paper) → propose move to 20-Sources/papers/ via bash mv; log to ai-changes.md. electron-density-topology.md (type=Concept, standalone idea) → draft Galaxy skeleton in response and notify user; do NOT write to 30-Galaxy/ without user approval. Report lists both actions. ai-changes.md updated.
